0: Who is so great a God as our God?
You are the God who do wonders!
Amongst the people have You made Your power known!
And I said: now have I made a beginning: this change has been wrought by the right hand of the Most High.
I have remembered the works of the Lord, and called to mind Your wonders from of old.
1: Our God is in heaven and on earth;
all things whatsoever He has willed, He has done.
When Israel went out of Egypt, and the house of Jacob from among a barbarous people, Judea became His sanctuary, Israel His dominion.
The sea beheld and fled, the Jordan turned back.
What ails you, O sea, that you fled? And you, O Jordan, that you turned back?
2: cry aloud to God that He may hear me.
In the day of my distress I sought the Lord.
My soul refused to be consoled.
Your ways, O God, are holy.
3: O God, listen to my prayer,
do not hide from my pleading.
For they bring down evil upon me.
As for me, I will cry to God, and the Lord will save me, evening, morning, and at noon.
4: I love You, Lord, my strength,
my rock, my fortress, my Saviour.
My God is the rock where I take refuge; my shield, my mighty help, my stronghold.
The Lord is worthy of all praise; when I call I am saved from my foes.
From His temple He heard my voice; my cry came to His ears.
deceased: Blessed are they whom You have chosen and taken to Yourself, O Lord.
Their memorial is from generation and generation.
Their souls shall dwell among good things.
6: Do not turn Your face away from your child, for I am afflicted; hear me quickly. Be attentive to my soul and deliver it.
Let Your salvation, Lord, help me.
Let the poor see it and be glad.
Seek God, and your souls shall live.
5: You have given an inheritance, O Lord, to those who fear You.
From the ends of the earth I have called to You.
I will hide in the shelter of Your wings.
Thus I will always chant to Your name.
